content:
{
  "title": "Function Signature-Conditioned Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation with LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often produce syntactically correct but semantically incorrect code, particularly when prompt context is insufficiently constrained. Traditional prompts may fail to focus generation on the intended function's purpose and interface.",
  "motivation": "Improving the semantic correctness of LLM-generated code is critical for adoption in software engineering workflows. With increasingly complex APIs and code synthesis needs, precise prompting techniques are necessary to guide models to more reliable solutions.",
  "method": "We propose a prompting method where the function signature and relevant docstring are presented in a dedicated, formatted block at the start of the prompt, followed by a natural-language user request. The LLM is instructed to generate code that conforms to the given signature, using a template such as:

```
Function signature:
def foo(bar: int) -> str:
"""Returns foo of bar."""
---
Task: Write the function implementation.
```
This explicit separation may help models better anchor their completions to the desired interface and intent. We hypothesize it will reduce off-spec and hallucinated outputs.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will test on HumanEval and MBPP, measuring pass@1 and pass@10. Baselines include standard natural-language prompts and code-only prompts. The key metric is the rate of correct, signature-conforming completions as judged by automated test cases and signature parsing."
}
